examples: Like her immediate predecessors, Zora Neale Hurston and Gwendolyn Brooks, she focused her novel on an ordinary Black woman's search for identity within the context of a Black community.
Zora's mother died in nineteen-oh-four, when Zora was thirteen years old.
Zora was twenty-six years old when she began high school. But she said she was only sixteen.
In 1975, writer Alice Walker wrote an essay about her search for Zora Neale Hurston.
But when Zora signals that she has detected explosives in a locked chest in one villager's house, there are grim faces all around and time stands still for us too.
